column3: Body
Go and have a look at the south fence — saw cattle drift across it yesterday.
Bores haven't been topped. Take the run before lunch.
Need you to push the mob out of Pat's East and back toward the river.
Pump on the Wallace Road bore was clicking. Have a look.
Check on the weaners — heard a few coughing.
Track out to Limestone's getting rutted. Grader's in the shed.
There's a calf adrift in Telephone North — see if you can get it back to mum.
Salt licks in the home paddock need topping up.
Run the eastern boundary fence before knock-off — feral pigs got through last week.
Trough at Pearcer's was nearly empty. Top it.
The horse-yard gate latch is busted. Spare's on the workbench.
Want a head count on the Mid II mob when you get a chance.
Neighbour reckons there's dogs working the Bull paddock. Take the rifle.
Get the rainwater tanks scrubbed before the wet — couple of weeks off.
Lay a length of poly pipe to the new trough before the rain hits.
The quad rack in the shed is loose. Sort it.
Hold the weaners in Holding paddock corner today — get 'em used to pressure.
Parkinsonia's spreading in the Back. Take the spray pack.
Mob's standing in the sun in Sandridge South — push them under the gums.
House tank's getting low. Check the pump on the way past.
